title: File size grew over 51% since last successful test baseline
description: Detects script files whose byte size has increased by more than 51% compared to the last known-good baseline captured after a successful test run. This catches content multiplication, runaway code generation, accidental paste duplication, and scope creep that may not trigger the hard 5MB SIN-PATTERN-013 threshold but still warrants investigation. The 51% threshold balances sensitivity with normal development churn.
detection_method: Compare current file size against temp/semisin-baseline.json. Trigger when (current - baseline) / baseline > 0.51
preventionRule: Review any file growing more than 51% in a single edit cycle. Check for duplicated blocks, runaway scaffolding, or agent-generated content bloat.
remedy: Diff the file against the last checkpoint or git commit. Identify and remove duplicated blocks. If growth is intentional, update the baseline with -UpdateBaseline.
